theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Energy (+0.81%) and Technology (+0.76%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-1.14%) and Materials (-0.99%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +0.11% versus -0.88% for defensives and -0.40% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.14%, Dow -0.40%, Nasdaq +0.41% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at -0.11%. The VIX fell 5.12% to 17.78, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold slipped 0.36% to $4,013.50, crude oil was little changed at $81.94, and bitcoin gained 1.19% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
